name: Portfolio concierge
greeting: I can guide you through Ka's strongest work, engineering evidence, startup portfolio, and collaboration path.
fallback: I could not verify a precise answer from the public portfolio. Try asking about a project, Ka's engineering workflow, his background, or collaboration.

starters.label: Understand the AI workflow
starters.query: How does Ka work with AI agents?

starters.label: Discuss an opportunity
starters.query: How can I collaborate with Ka?

topics.title: Where to start
topics.answer: Start with Chomkar for product delivery under real constraints, Studio OS for human-agent systems and privacy boundaries, and the AI-Native Team Starter for public code, tests, and reusable engineering governance. Together they show product judgment, implementation, and a workflow other people can inspect.
followUps: How does Studio OS protect private work?

topics.title: About Ka Vatana
topics.answer: Ka Vatana is a Cambodian Year 1 Software Engineering student and founder-engineer. He builds Cambodia-first products, leads CHNAI LAB, and uses public case studies and runnable checks to document what is shipped, simulated, private, and still being validated.

topics.title: AI-native engineering
phrases: how does ka work with ai agents
topics.answer: Ka uses agents for research, implementation, review, and repetitive verification, while humans retain product intent, access decisions, factual judgment, and merge authority. The workflow records scope, evidence, AI involvement, risk, rollback, and human verification in the repository instead of treating generated output as trusted by default.
followUps: What can agents never do automatically?

topics.title: Runnable engineering evidence
topics.answer: The clearest public code proof is the AI-Native Team Starter: a reusable template with a zero-dependency readiness scanner, regression tests, immutable GitHub Actions pins, and a public release. This portfolio is also a Nuxt application whose repository policy, claims, media references, credential patterns, types, lint, dependencies, tests, and static build run through one verification command.

topics.title: Seven startup tracks
topics.answer: The seven tracks are BayonHub for student opportunities, Svaeng Yul for Khmer tech learning, Chomkar for agricultural market access, Sat Digital for community cybersecurity, Vantrex for trading tools, PHSAROS for SME operations, and CHNAI LAB as the student-run product studio coordinating the work. Product source remains private while approved case studies and status claims stay public.
followUps: Why are the startup repositories private?

topics.title: Public and private boundary
topics.answer: Future-startup source, credentials, user data, private strategy, and the Studio OS operator remain private. Public surfaces expose only approved case studies, synthetic or redacted evidence, reusable tooling, and claims that can be checked without granting visitors or public agents access to Ka's private digital workspace.
followUps: How does the public concierge stay isolated?
What can agents never do automatically?

topics.title: Education and learning
topics.answer: Ka is a Year 1 Software Engineering student at BELTEI International University and a Year 1 English student at IFL. His public journey also records earlier mathematics competitions, STEM activities, volunteering, teaching, marketing work, and product-building experience.
followUps: What has Ka built outside class?

topics.title: Collaboration and opportunities
topics.answer: Ka is open to internships, collaborations, and serious learning opportunities involving product work, front-end or full-stack development, AI-native workflows, and Cambodia-relevant technology. The direct path is the contact page or LinkedIn; include the problem, expected outcome, timeline, and how you want to work together.
followUps: What kind of work fits Ka best?

topics.title: Human authority
topics.answer: Agents may retrieve bounded context, prepare work, run allowlisted checks, and produce evidence. Merges, deployments, access changes, external messages, destructive operations, security response, payments, financial decisions, and trading actions remain human-controlled unless a future capability is separately authenticated, scoped, confirmed, and audited.
